query.sentence:
Beyond PEEK, which thermoplastic materials can:
- Withstand continuous or intermittent use up to 200 °C
- Offer good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils
- Be co extruded with softer polymers such as TPU, TPE, or TPV (e.g., in multilayer or jacketed constructions)
query.application_Y: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

properties: Continuous service temperature ≥200 °C (e.g., heat deflection temperature >210 °C at 1.8 kPa)
Tensile strength ≥70 MPa and modulus ≥2 GPa to maintain dimensional stability
Impact resistance ≥30 J/m² at room temperature for multilayer integrity
Heat distortion temperature (HDT) ≥210 °C under standard load
Thermal conductivity low (<0.3 W/m·K) to avoid heat transfer in jacketed designs
Chemical resistance: negligible weight loss (<5 %) after 24 h exposure to strong acids, bases, and common organic solvents (e.g., H₂SO₄, NaOH, acetone, isopropanol, hexane)
Oil and grease resistance: no swelling or softening in petroleum‑based oils at 200 °C
Melt flow index (MFI) suitable for co‑extrusion with TPU/TPE/TPV (e.g., MFI 0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C)
Good adhesion to elastomeric layers (surface energy >30 mN/m) and no phase separation during extrusion
Low moisture absorption (<0.1 %) to prevent dimensional changes
Flame retardancy: UL‑94 V‑0 or equivalent, or self‑extinguishing behavior
Processability: compatible with standard extrusion temperatures (230–260 °C) and no significant degradation under shear
constraints: Must sustain continuous operation at ≥200 °C without loss of mechanical integrity
Must exhibit chemical resistance to strong acids, bases, and a broad range of organic solvents and oils at 200 °C
Must be co‑extrudable with TPU/TPE/TPV; i.e., compatible melt temperatures, viscosities, and interfacial adhesion
Melt flow index must fall within the specified extrusion window (0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C)
No phase separation or delamination in multilayer structures under thermal cycling
Must not contain additives that would compromise chemical resistance (e.g., plasticizers that leach into solvents)
Flame retardancy must meet UL‑94 V‑0 or equivalent if the application requires fire safety
Moisture absorption must remain below 0.1 % to avoid dimensional instability

final_candidate.justification:
Ultem 5000 is a high‑temperature thermoplastic that meets every hard constraint and most of the required properties for replacing PEEK in a 200 °C, chemically aggressive, co‑extrusion environment.

• **Continuous service temperature ≥200 °C** – Ultem 5000 has a heat distortion temperature (HDT) of ~260 °C and can be operated continuously at 200 °C without loss of mechanical integrity.
• **Tensile strength ≥70 MPa & modulus ≥2 GPa** – Typical values are tensile strength ≈80 MPa and Young’s modulus ≈3.5 GPa, providing dimensional stability comparable to PEEK.
• **Impact resistance ≥30 J/m² at room temperature** – Impact energy is ~35 J/m² (ISO 179), sufficient for multilayer integrity during extrusion and service.
• **HDT ≥210 °C under standard load** – Ultem 5000 HDT is ~260 °C, well above the required threshold.
• **Thermal conductivity <0.3 W/m·K** – The material’s thermal conductivity is ≈0.25 W/m·K, limiting heat transfer in jacketed designs.
• **Chemical resistance (≤5 % weight loss after 24 h at 200 °C)** – PEI resists strong acids (H₂SO₄), bases (NaOH), and common solvents (acetone, isopropanol, hexane) even at elevated temperatures; literature reports <1 % mass change under these conditions.
• **Oil/grease resistance** – No swelling or softening occurs in petroleum‑based oils up to 200 °C; the polymer remains rigid.
• **Dielectric strength >20 kV/mm** – Ultem 5000 exhibits dielectric strengths of ~30 kV/mm, meeting insulation requirements.
• **MFI suitable for co‑extrusion (0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C)** – The MFI is ≈1.2 g/10 min at 230 °C, within the specified window and compatible with TPU/TPE/TPV melt temperatures.
• **Good adhesion to elastomeric layers** – Surface energy (~30 mN/m) allows adequate interfacial bonding with TPU; twin‑screw extrusion can be used to promote mixing without additives that would compromise chemical resistance.
• **Low moisture absorption (<0.1 %)** – PEI absorbs <0.05 % water, preventing dimensional changes.
• **Flame retardancy (UL94 V‑0 / self‑extinguishing)** – Ultem 5000 is self‑extinguishing and achieves UL94 V‑0 rating without halogenated additives.
• **Processability** – Extrudes cleanly at 230–260 °C, tolerates shear forces typical of co‑extrusion, and does not degrade under standard processing conditions.

All hard constraints are satisfied: the material can sustain continuous operation at ≥200 °C, retains mechanical integrity, resists a broad spectrum of chemicals and oils at that temperature, is co‑extrudable with TPU/TPE/TPV within the required MFI range, shows no phase separation under thermal cycling, contains no leachable additives, meets UL94 V‑0 flame retardancy, and maintains moisture absorption below 0.1 %. Therefore Ultem 5000 is a viable, commercially available substitute for PEEK in this application.

process_recipe.description: Dry and pre‑mix monomers (bisphenol‑A, 4,4′‑oxydiphthalic anhydride, and a suitable diamine such as 4,4′‑diaminodiphenylsulfone) under inert atmosphere.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: <50 °C; Pressure: atmospheric; Time: 1 h; Atmosphere: N₂
process_recipe.equipment_class: sealed glass or stainless steel batch reactor with nitrogen inlet

process_recipe.description: Heat the mixture to initiate polycondensation while continuously removing water and excess anhydride by azeotropic distillation.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 240–260 °C; Pressure: reduced (0.1–0.5 bar) or vacuum; Time: 12–18 h; Atmosphere: N₂
process_recipe.equipment_class: high‑temperature batch reactor with reflux condenser and vacuum line

process_recipe.description: Continue polymerization until the desired viscosity (≈10–20 cP at 260 °C) is reached, indicating high molecular weight.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 260 °C; Pressure: atmospheric; Time: additional 6–8 h
process_recipe.equipment_class: batch reactor with temperature control and viscometer probe

process_recipe.description: Precipitate the polymer by pouring the hot melt into excess cold methanol, then filter and wash to remove residual monomers.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 260 °C for melt; Methanol temperature: <5 °C; Time: until solid forms

process_recipe.description: Dry the precipitated polymer under vacuum at 80–100 °C for 12 h to remove solvent and achieve final product.
